---
species: "Modiolus modiolus"
accepted_code: "MOMO"
alternate_codes: []
diminutive: #"CODE" # uncomment and fill in if one exists
alternate_diminutives: ["", ""]
common_name: "Modiolus modiolus"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Large bivalve with a thick shell and a glossy yellow or brown coloring." # make sure to fill this in!
image: '/header_images/modiolus_modiolus.jpg'
categories:
  - bivalves
sampling_programs:
    - keen_upc
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
habitat:
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 140467
---

![](/header_images/modiolus_modiolus.jpg)
Photo from IMARES via [WORMS](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=image&pic=1984&tid=140467)

**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Larger and faster growing than other similar species. It has a thick shell and a glossy yellow or brown coloring, and a flat umbo. It is also ypically found subtidally rather than intertidally.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** Mytilus edilus (MYED)

<br/>

**Extended Description:** Large, thick, glossy yellow-brown bivalve. Attaches to hard substrate using byssal threads, typically in deeper, colder, saltier waters. They are less suited to the intertidal, particularly the upper intertidal.

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=140467#images)**
